checks.finding: T-001, T-006, T-007, and T-008 declare medium risk for limited, controlled, behavioral repository changes. T-002 through T-005 declare high risk for sensitive approval, immutable-input, agent-lifecycle, and independent-gate boundaries, consistent with their structured Risk Impact, Risk Reversibility, and Risk Surface fields. No task scope modifies authentication, payments, PII storage, data migration, or an externally visible API contract at an insufficient tier.

Each high-risk task T-002, T-003, T-004, and T-005 includes exact Red→Green evidence paths with failing tests committed before implementation, plus a named independent reviewer verdict recorded in its implementation report. No critical-risk task exists.

T-001 through T-008 are coherent focused units, each with no more than three closely related implementation areas and four to six Done When items. The decomposition separates capability declarations, routing, immutable manifests, implementation isolation, review isolation, reporting, release synchronization, and rollback without single-edit fragmentation.

Functional tasks have explicit negative-path acceptance evidence: T-001 and T-002 map to AC-001 fail-closed model/runtime/risk and terminal-resume cases; T-003 maps to AC-003 and AC-004 tampering and malformed-provenance rejection; T-004 and T-005 map to AC-002 identity-reuse, missing-manifest, unlisted-input, hash-mismatch, and chat-only rejection; T-006 maps to AC-005 legacy compatibility and rejection of missing current-schema fields. T-007 and T-008 are release/infrastructure tasks covered by AC-006 and AC-007.

No task is declared as a standalone unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance test task. Embedded verification is appropriately scoped: TEST-001 covers routing behavior, TEST-002 crosses orchestration and identity boundaries, TEST-003 validates manifest integrity across paired runtimes, and TEST-004 through TEST-006 reference their mapped acceptance criteria.

Every high-risk task T-002 through T-005 has an explicit Rollback field naming restoration or removal through the pinned rollback transaction, and each Done When section includes an isolated rollback fixture.

Primary implementation ownership is separated across T-001 capability declarations, T-002 selectors/resume guards, T-003 manifest mechanics, T-004 implementation orchestration, T-005 reviewer/evaluator gates, T-006 reporting, T-007 release surfaces, and T-008 rollback tooling. Where orchestration or release policy is sequentially related, Blockers and explicit Out of Scope sections coordinate the work.

The dependency chain is logically supported: T-002 and T-003 consume T-001 capability definitions; T-004 consumes routing and manifest outputs from T-002 and T-003; T-005 consumes T-003 manifest boundaries; T-006 records identity and gate outcomes from T-004 and T-005; T-007 synchronizes release surfaces after all feature components; and T-008 requires T-007 final release hashes for its rollback inventory. No missing or spurious blocker is evident.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.
